title: First Real Match
prose: The reserve spot stops being theoretical the moment a first-choice player turns up to breakfast with a broken wrist and a sheepish look, and you find yourself being handed real kit, in front of a real crowd, with roughly four hours' warning.

It's over almost before your nerves catch up with what's happening — a blur of cold air and shouted instructions and one genuinely good play you'll be replaying in your head for weeks. Whoever wins, you were actually out there for it.

choices.label: You more than held your own.
You survived it, mostly — and that's enough for now.
